auth_type: Basic Auth or Static API Key
auth_notes: Most legacy Document Management Systems rely on Basic Authentication or a static token passed via custom headers. Some older systems may require a SOAP-based login request to retrieve a session ID/cookie for subsequent calls.
rate_limits: Typically unmanaged or restricted by server hardware capacity; often lacks standard 429 headers.
gotchas: Responses are often in XML/SOAP format requiring heavy use of the XML node or custom JavaScript for parsing.
File uploads often require specific multipart/form-data formatting that is sensitive to boundary definitions.
Lack of standardized error handling; systems may return 200 OK with an error message in the response body.
client_must_provide: On-premise API Gateway URL or VPN access
WSDL or Swagger/OpenAPI documentation if available
research_notes: Implementation will require the HTTP Request node. If the system is SOAP-based, the developer must manually construct XML envelopes in the body. For file handling, the 'Always Output Data' and 'Binary Data' settings in n8n will be critical to manage buffers between the DMS and other nodes.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation will require the HTTP Request node. If the system is SOAP-based, the developer must manually construct XML envelopes in the body. For file handling, the 'Always Output Data' and 'Binary Data' settings in n8n will be critical to manage buffers between the DMS and other nodes.
